description: Query autocomplete suggestions from search engines for keyword research

query.help: Get autocomplete suggestions for a query.

arguments.name: query
arguments.help: Search query to get suggestions for

options.name: --source
options.help: [google|youtube|bing|amazon|ddg]  Sources to query (google, youtube, bing, amazon, ddg). Can be repeated

options.name: --depth
options.help: Recursion depth (how many levels deep to query)

options.name: --recurse-limit
options.help: Max suggestions to recurse into per level (default: 5)

examples: keywords suggest query "best laptop" --source google --source youtube
keywords suggest query "seo tools" -s google -s amazon -t
query.usage_instructions: Get keyword suggestions from search engine autocomplete. QUERY is required (positional). Use --source/-s to select engines (google, youtube, bing, amazon, ddg -- can repeat for multiple). Use --filter/-f to only show suggestions containing specific text. Use --recurse/-r with --depth/-d to recursively expand suggestions (default depth: 1, limit: 5 per level). Use --limit/-l (default: 100), --table/-t.

sources.usage_instructions: Show which search engines are available as autocomplete sources. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

suggest.usage_instructions: Keyword suggestion tools -- query autocomplete APIs and list available sources.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `keywords cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `keywords cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

usage_instructions: Use for keyword research by querying autocomplete suggestions from multiple search engines (Google, YouTube, Bing, Amazon, DuckDuckGo). Supports recursive expansion of suggestions. Commands follow `keywords suggest <action>` pattern.
